name: Reactive Readiness Protocol
why: Primes the nervous system for explosive movements through progressive reactivity.

application.headline: A progressive sequence — from self-paced reactivity to high-intensity start/stop work.
steps: Start with self-paced reactive shuffles to coach's pointing.
Progress to partner ball tosses in athletic stance.
Add quick drop-and-stick jumps focusing on landing mechanics.

science: Gradually increasing reactive demands optimises the stretch-shortening cycle and prepares the neuromuscular junction for high-intensity training.
